content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, FARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
SACRAMENTO                          591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000          SAN FRANCISCO
770 L STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814        MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941        650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752            TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800             SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
                                                                           TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800


                                    July 3, 1991


John W. Wallace, Esq.
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

     Re:  Contributions to Officeholder Accounts

Dear John:

     Recently, I asked several questions regarding the
application of the "transfer ban" at Government Code section
85304.  My inquiry was prompted by the Commission's recent Advice
Letter to Frederick K. Lowell (I-91-064, 2/6/91) in which the
Commission advised, in part:

          "Moreover, section 85304 continues to
          prohibit candidates for state or local office
          from transferring campaign funds to any
          candidate who is subject to any valid
          contribution limits, including those limits
          established by local jurisdictions."

     I advised you that we represent San Francisco Mayor Art
Agnos.  San Francisco does have a valid contribution limit
ordinance of $500 per election.  However, the City Attorney has
opined that a committee maintained after a successful election
effort and controlled by a San Francisco officeholder, which is
not for re-election purposes, is not subject to the contribution
limitation ordinance.  These so-called "Officeholder" or
"Friends" accounts, while not subject to San Francisco limits,
remain subject to the Political Reform Act (PRA).  Mayor Agnos
has two candidate-controlled campaign committees: Friends of Art
Agnos, I.D. #747235 which is his officeholder account from
his 1987 campaign, and Agnos For Mayor, I.D. #903014 which is his
1991 re-election account.  The first committee is not subject to
San Francisco contribution limitations, the second is.

     Based upon the Commission's advice, referenced above, the
Agnos for Mayor Committee has returned all contributions received
from other California state or local candidates and has advised
those candidates of the Commission's interpretation that such
contributions are not permitted.

John W. Wallace, Esq.
July 3, 1991
Page 2

     In our telephone conversation, I asked several additional
questions regarding the types of campaign contributions which
could be received from other "candidates" for the Mayor account
and the Friends account.  Your responses, as I understood them,
are as follows:

1.   Q:   May a candidate for state or local office make a
          personal contribution or a contribution from his or her
          controlled committee to Friends?

     A:   Yes.  There is no "transfer ban" provision for personal
          contributions, and committee contributions are also
          unlimited as long as the Friends account is not used as
          a conduit to the Mayor account since that account is
          under a valid local limit.

2.   Q:   May a candidate for federal office (not presently a
          California state or local public official) or a non-
          California candidate make a contribution to Mayor or
          Friends from campaign funds?

     A:   Yes.  The transfer ban at Government Code section 85304
          would not apply to such candidates or their committees.

3.   Q:   Can a state candidate, who has been or who is
          concurrently a federal candidate, make a contribution
          to Mayor or Friends from that candidate's federal
          campaign account?

     A:   An unlimited contribution can be made to the Friends
          account, but a contribution to the Mayor account would
          violate the transfer ban since the transfer ban
          prohibits any state or local candidate from
          transft writing funds (even from a federal committee) to
          any other candidate where there is a valid contribution
          limit.

     We would appreciate your written confirmation of our
telephone conversation as described above.  If you have any
questions, or need any additional information, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

                                        Kind regards,


                                        Kirk Alan Pessner
                                        Paralegal/Administrator
                                        Political Reports Unit

KAP/df
cc:  Thomas B. Isaak
     Kia Currie
     #6218.02
